```java
class LFUCache {
    private class Node {
        int key, value, count;
        Node prev, next;
        Node(int key, int value) {
            this.key = key;
            this.value = value;
            this.count = 1;
        }
    }

    private class DoublyLinkedList {
        Node head, tail;
        int size;
        DoublyLinkedList() {
            head = new Node(0, 0);
            tail = new Node(0, 0);
            head.next = tail;
            tail.prev = head;
        }

        void add(Node node) {
            node.next = head.next;
            node.prev = head;
            head.next.prev = node;
            head.next = node;
            size++;
        }

        void remove(Node node) {
            node.prev.next = node.next;
            node.next.prev = node.prev;
            size--;
        }

        Node removeTail() {
            Node node = tail.prev;
            remove(node);
            return node;
        }
    }

    private int capacity, size;
    private Map<Integer, Node> keyMap;
    private Map<Integer, DoublyLinkedList> freqMap;
    private int minFreq;

    public LFUCache(int capacity) {
        this.capacity = capacity;
        this.size = 0;
        keyMap = new HashMap<>();
        freqMap = new HashMap<>();
        minFreq = 0;
    }

    public int get(int key) {
        if (!keyMap.containsKey(key)) return -1;
        Node node = keyMap.get(key);
        updateNode(node);
        return node.value;
    }

    public void put(int key, int value) {
        if (capacity == 0) return;
        if (keyMap.containsKey(key)) {
            Node node = keyMap.get(key);
            node.value = value;
            updateNode(node);
        } else {
            if (size == capacity) {
                DoublyLinkedList list = freqMap.get(minFreq);
                Node nodeToRemove = list.removeTail();
                keyMap.remove(nodeToRemove.key);
                if (list.size == 0) freqMap.remove(minFreq);
                size--;
            }
            Node node = new Node(key, value);
            keyMap.put(key, node);
            freqMap.putIfAbsent(1, new DoublyLinkedList());
            freqMap.get(1).add(node);
            minFreq = 1;
            size++;
        }
    }

    private void updateNode(Node node) {
        int freq = node.count++;
        DoublyLinkedList oldList = freqMap.get(freq);
        oldList.remove(node);
        if (oldList.size == 0) {
            freqMap.remove(freq);
            if (minFreq == freq) minFreq++;
        }
        freqMap.putIfAbsent(node.count, new DoublyLinkedList());
        freqMap.get(node.count).add(node);
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two hash maps and doubly linked lists to maintain frequency and recency of access. Key operations (get and put) are optimized to O(1) by efficiently updating the data structures. The main strength is the use of frequency-based eviction with tie-breaking by recency. The weakness is the increased memory usage due to maintaining multiple data structures.
